name: Dream Cycle
description: Primary cognitive workflow that drives speculative discovery and validation across the knowledge graph.

steps.action: AWAKE → collect graph seeds and runtime metrics
steps.description: Gather current graph state, tensions, and runtime signals needed to start a cognitive cycle.

steps.action: REM → propose speculative edges via LLM
steps.description: Generate candidate relationships and hypotheses from the available evidence using model-assisted reasoning.

steps.action: NORMALIZING → validate edges against evidence and ADR guard rails
steps.description: Validate proposed edges against available evidence, accepted ADR constraints, and confidence thresholds before solidifying them.

name: Workflow Execution Process
description: This process executes defined workflows within the DreamGraph server, coordinating the various steps involved in a workflow. It ensures that workflows are carried out efficiently and correctly.

steps.action: Receive Workflow Request
steps.description: Capture the request to execute a specific workflow.

steps.action: Validate Workflow
steps.description: Check if the requested workflow is valid and can be executed.

steps.action: Execute Workflow Steps
steps.description: Carry out each step of the workflow in sequence.

steps.action: Return Workflow Result
steps.description: Provide the results of the workflow execution back to the user.

name: Project Onboarding and Enrichment
description: High-level onboarding workflow that scans the repository, bootstraps or enriches the fact graph, and rebuilds indexable architectural memory for later querying and cognition.

steps.action: Discover repository files and coarse project structure.
steps.description: Enumerate project files and derive an initial structural picture of the repository.

steps.action: Generate a first-pass graph using init_graph when no seed memory exists or a reset is required.
steps.description: Initialize baseline graph memory when the project has not yet been seeded or must be rebuilt.

steps.action: Run scan_project to attempt semantic extraction of features, workflows, and data model entities.
steps.description: Scan the codebase to extract structured knowledge candidates for the graph.

steps.action: Merge curated or manually derived knowledge into the seed graph through enrich_seed_data.
steps.description: Upsert curated features, workflows, and data-model entities into the graph seed data.

steps.action: Rebuild indexes and expose resulting resources for query and downstream cognitive use.
steps.description: Refresh graph indexes and resource views so the newly captured knowledge becomes queryable and usable by later cognition.

name: VS Code Assisted Reasoning
description: Brings DreamGraph context into the editor through extension-hosted panels, status indicators, daemon communication, and contextual prompt building.
trigger: VS Code extension activation or user interaction

steps.action: Activate the VS Code extension and establish daemon communication.
steps.description: Start the extension and connect to the DreamGraph daemon so editor features have live project context.

steps.action: Build or retrieve relevant DreamGraph context for the current editing situation.
steps.description: Collect contextual project knowledge relevant to the active file, selection, or user intent.

steps.action: Render conversational or dashboard surfaces inside VS Code.
steps.description: Show DreamGraph-driven chat or dashboard UI inside the extension host surfaces.

steps.action: Let the user ask questions, inspect context, and monitor DreamGraph state from the editor.
steps.description: Support interactive reasoning and system inspection directly from the IDE.

steps.action: Reflect daemon health and status through lightweight UI signals such as the status bar.
steps.description: Expose connectivity and health state so the user can see whether DreamGraph services are available.

name: Discipline Task Lifecycle
description: Tracks how a discipline task is created, progressed, reviewed, and closed while keeping ownership and status transitions explicit.
trigger: task creation or discipline status transition

steps.action: Create a discipline task with scope and owner.
steps.description: Open a task record that captures the requested work, responsible discipline, and initial constraints.

steps.action: Prioritize and schedule the task.
steps.description: Place the task into the discipline backlog or active queue according to urgency and dependencies.

steps.action: Execute the task within the assigned discipline context.
steps.description: Perform the work while preserving the discipline-specific tools, prompts, and boundaries.

steps.action: Review results and capture follow-up actions.
steps.description: Assess whether the task outcome satisfies the intent and whether more work must be spawned.

steps.action: Update task status and evidence.
steps.description: Record completion notes, evidence, or blockers so later sessions can understand the state.

steps.action: Close or hand off the task.
steps.description: Finish the lifecycle by marking the task complete or transferring ownership for the next stage.

name: Semantic UI Registration Flow
description: Captures semantic UI elements, their data contracts, interaction model, and platform implementations so interface concepts can be queried and migrated independently of rendering technology.

steps.action: Define a semantic UI element with purpose, category, inputs, outputs, and interactions.
steps.description: Describe the UI concept independently of any concrete rendering technology.

steps.action: Optionally associate child semantic elements and known platform implementations.
steps.description: Capture composition relationships and any current implementations across platforms.

steps.action: Register or merge the element into the UI registry.
steps.description: Persist the semantic element so it becomes part of the shared UI knowledge base.

steps.action: Query elements by feature, category, platform, or migration gaps.
steps.description: Retrieve registered UI concepts to support search, inspection, and migration analysis.

steps.action: Use registered elements to support migration planning and architectural documentation.
steps.description: Apply registry knowledge to broader UI planning and documentation workflows.

name: Scheduled Cognitive Execution
description: Automates recurring cognitive actions like dream cycles, nightmare scans, metacognitive analysis, and narrative updates according to temporal triggers.

steps.action: Create a schedule with an action type and trigger configuration.
steps.description: Define the recurring cognitive action together with the timing or event trigger that should fire it.

steps.action: Persist and enable the schedule.
steps.description: Store the schedule so it survives process restarts and becomes eligible for execution.

steps.action: When the trigger condition is met, execute the configured cognitive action subject to safety guards.
steps.description: Run the scheduled cognitive operation only when timing and safety conditions allow it.

steps.action: Record execution history including success or failure.
steps.description: Capture an audit trail of each scheduled run and whether it completed successfully.

steps.action: Allow later inspection, update, manual run, or deletion of the schedule.
steps.description: Support ongoing management of the scheduled cognitive action after creation.

name: VS Code Autonomy Continuation Loop
description: After each architect pass, the extension extracts the structured pass envelope, derives recommended actions, analyzes continuation safety against autonomy mode and pass budget, updates visible autonomy status, and may self-continue with a synthesized next prompt until the goal is reached, risk rises, or the budget is exhausted.
trigger: assistant pass completion in the VS Code architect chat panel

steps.action: Detect requested/configured autonomy mode and pass budget
steps.description: Read the current autonomy settings so continuation decisions stay within configured limits.

steps.action: Inject reporting/autonomy instruction blocks into the architect system prompt
steps.description: Ensure the assistant response format includes the structured continuation envelope needed by the extension.

steps.action: Extract structured pass envelope and recommended next actions from the response
steps.description: Parse the assistant output to recover progress, uncertainty, and action recommendations.

steps.action: Analyze progress, uncertainty, and scope to decide continuation
steps.description: Determine whether the session should continue automatically or stop based on policy and current state.

steps.action: Broadcast autonomy status and actions to the webview
steps.description: Update the visible UI so the user can see the autonomy state and proposed next steps.

steps.action: If allowed, synthesize the next prompt and run another bounded pass
steps.description: Perform another controlled assistant turn only when autonomy mode and safety checks permit it.

name: CLI Enrich Pipeline
description: The dg enrich workflow resolves the target instance, verifies a running daemon, optionally scans the project, runs enrich_seed_data, and reports graph coverage improvements.

steps.action: Resolve target instance and daemon metadata
steps.description: Identify the instance to enrich and gather the daemon connection details needed for the operation.

steps.action: Validate daemon is reachable and has a port
steps.description: Confirm the target daemon is running and can accept enrichment-related requests.

steps.action: Run scan_project unless skip-scan is enabled
steps.description: Optionally refresh structural and semantic understanding before enrichment begins.

steps.action: Run enrich_seed_data unless skip-enrich is enabled
steps.description: Upsert seed knowledge into the graph so the instance becomes more complete and queryable.

steps.action: Report scan and enrichment summaries in text or JSON
steps.description: Return a usable summary of the operation and the resulting graph coverage changes.

name: CLI Curate Audit
description: The dg curate workflow inspects graph quality on a running daemon by querying features, workflows, data model entities, and ADRs, then groups findings and suggests next actions. If no daemon is running, it emits advisory-only guidance.

steps.action: Resolve target instance and determine whether the daemon is running
steps.description: Figure out which instance to audit and whether live graph inspection is possible.

steps.action: If no daemon is present, emit advisory guidance only
steps.description: Fall back to non-live guidance when the system cannot query a running daemon.

steps.action: Query live graph resources for requested quality targets
steps.description: Inspect the live graph for workflows, features, data model entities, and ADR coverage.

steps.action: Detect stale, weak, empty, or underspecified records
steps.description: Identify graph quality gaps that should be curated or remediated.

steps.action: Group findings and print suggested remediation actions
steps.description: Present the audit output in a way that guides the next graph-improvement steps.

name: Architect Agentic Tool Execution
description: Executes architect chat requests through a structured tool-calling loop and renders rich execution artifacts in the VS Code chat surface.
trigger: User submits a request in the VS Code architect chat panel.

steps.action: Accept user prompt and optional multimodal attachments.
steps.description: Capture the architect request together with any attached context such as images or files.

steps.action: Normalize provider-specific request payloads for the active LLM provider.
steps.description: Translate the request into the provider-specific shape needed for the selected model backend.

steps.action: Execute structured tool-calling loop under architect orchestration.
steps.description: Run the orchestrated reasoning loop that can call tools and iterate toward an answer.

steps.action: Render verdicts, traces, pills, and other advanced execution artifacts immediately in the structured webview.
steps.description: Display rich execution outputs as soon as they are available rather than waiting for a plain final answer.

steps.action: Render recognized file and entity references as interactive links.
steps.description: Convert recognized references into navigable IDE links to improve follow-up exploration.

steps.action: Preserve the end-to-end execution loop while progressively updating the rich chat surface.
steps.description: Keep the tool-execution lifecycle coherent while continuously improving the user-visible chat surface.
